factors.name: Dow earnings & guidance
factors.summary: SoftBank surging 11%+ on AI optimism, Lenovo +13% on AI earnings; Boeing eying delivery rebound. Mixed signal—AI tailwinds vs. aerospace/industrial headwinds (Boeing delays, SpaceX scrubs).

factors.name: Fed policy & rate expectations
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed chair Friday (hawkish lean vs Blankfein era). Rising talk of hawkish Fed tone per FXStreet; USD at 6-week high (DXY 99.25). Rate-sensitive Dow names (utilities, dividend payers) under pressure.

factors.name: US-Iran ceasefire progress & oil
factors.summary: Oil down ~$1 on peace talk optimism (WTI $96.80, Brent mid-$80s expected per Bloomberg survey). Eases energy shock inflation fears and reduces geopolitical risk premium. Supports consumer & transport costs.

factors.name: Japan CPI & BOJ
factors.summary: Japan core CPI 1.4% YoY (4-year low), well below 1.7% forecast, but ING still expects June BOJ hike. Soft inflation may delay BoJ tightening vs. near-term expectations; mixed for yen and global growth.

factors.name: China data & FDI policy
factors.summary: NDRC softens tone on foreign tech investment (no outright ban), but China's National Team cutting ETF stakes 90% in H1 2026—mixed liquidity signal. Caterpillar & materials demand outlook unclear.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD at 6-week high (DXY ~99.25) pressures multinational earnings translation; Dow has ~50% international revenue. EUR/USD weak at 1.1610, USD/JPY at 159.10; dollar carry-trade tightening.

factors.name: US labor & growth momentum
factors.summary: US resilient per 'robust labour data' note; Australia jobless rate up (RBA hike bets fade). Mixed global growth backdrop—no fresh US PMI/ISM data in this window.

factors.name: Geopolitical tensions
factors.summary: Trump sends 5,000 more troops to Poland (week after cancelling 4,000); Iran nuclear deal fragile; Alberta referendum on separation looms. Elevated tail risks & policy uncertainty.

factors.name: Valuation & market structure
factors.summary: MarketWatch warns 'Buffett Indicator' signals market top (230% valuation gap); technical sell signals flashing despite Nvidia optimism. Fragility narrative building despite upside narrative.

factors.name: Price action momentum
factors.summary: US30 +0.23% in 4h, +0.53% in 24h—modest rally underway but not yet extended. Move is in-progress (Phase 2), requires retest to confirm strength.

screening.narrative: The Dow is in a modest Phase 2 rally (+0.53% in 24h) on US-Iran ceasefire optimism and select mega-cap AI wins (SoftBank +11%, Lenovo +13%), offsetting hawkish Fed chair Warsh signals and persistent dollar strength (DXY 99.25). Japan's unexpectedly soft CPI (1.4% vs 1.7% expected) eases near-term BoJ tightening fears but also signals global growth slowness. However, valuation warnings ('Buffett Indicator' signals excess) and elevated geopolitical risk (troops to Poland, Iran deal fragility, Alberta referendum) create a conflict between bullish tactical catalysts and bearish structural headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: US Michigan Consumer Sentiment (May; due Friday AM before market close at 2 PM ET for bonds)
Memorial Day (markets closed Monday 5/25; shortened Friday; full reopening Tuesday 5/26)
RBNZ rate decision (May 27—expected hold at 2.25%, but Q3 hike expectations rising)
Dow component earnings flow (BA, CAT, GE, JPM, V guidance in coming weeks)
Fed Warsh inaugural commentary/signals (new chair tone-setting)
China macro (any PMI/growth data releases; NDRC/PBOC policy signals)
Oil/energy market developments (Iran deal closure vs. stall-out could swing +/- $10/barrel)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran talks collapse or geopolitical escalation spikes, oil could spike >$110/bbl, triggering inflation fears and Flight-to-Quality (bonds rally, equities dump). Conversely, if Fed Warsh cuts rates faster than expected or US-Iran deal finalizes, Dow rallies hard through 50,500. Valuation risk (Buffett Indicator warning) means upside is capped if growth disappoints or tech earnings disappoint. China's ETF liquidation may trigger unexpected liquidity drain.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (underway); wait for pullback retest of 50,150–50,200 support to confirm strength before buying. On break above 50,500, reassess—extended moves often fizzle. Avoid chasing here; risk/reward favors patience into Memorial Day holiday gap and next major catalyst (BoJ/PMI/earnings).

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Optimism
factors.summary: US Secretary of State Rubio signaled 'good signs' for agreement; multiple sources report draft proposals exchanged and mediation intensifying. Risk-on sentiment supports equity demand.

factors.name: Oil Price Pressure & Strait of Hormuz Risk
factors.summary: WTI trading mid-$96s with reports of lingering uranium stockpile disputes and Hormuz closure risks. Commerzbank warns crude could spike above recent highs if Strait remains closed; energy cost headwinds for industrial/transport heavy Dow names.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength (6-week high)
factors.summary: USD holding near 99.50 DXY on hawkish Fed expectations despite Iran peace optimism. Strong dollar pressures multinational earnings translation for ~50% Dow international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Stagflation Concerns & UK/EU Weakness
factors.summary: UK retail sales fell 1.3% MoM vs -0.6% expected; France business confidence at Feb 2021 lows; Danske Bank notes stagflation fears rising. European recession risks may dampen global demand for Dow industrials (CAT, BA, GE).

factors.name: Tech/AI Sector Momentum (Nvidia, SoftBank, Lenovo)
factors.summary: Nvidia earnings focus positive, SoftBank gained $61B market cap, Lenovo surged 15% on AI revenue doubling. Broad-based AI enthusiasm extends to large-cap names; however, not a Dow-heavy driver relative to industrials.

factors.name: Global Equity Risk Sentiment (Asia strength, EM gains)
factors.summary: Japan Nikkei +2.74%, Asian stocks rising, EM equities on track for weekly gain. Positive spillover on easing geopolitical jitters and peace deal hopes supports US equity flows.

factors.name: Japan CPI & BOJ Rate Hike Signal
factors.summary: Japan core inflation fell to 4-year low (1.5% vs 1.7% expected); however, ING expects June BOJ hike anyway due to pipeline price pressures. Tighter global monetary conditions weigh on risk appetite.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations (year-end)
factors.summary: Markets pricing higher probability of Fed hike by year-end on hawkish sentiment; real yields rising. Pressures dividend-heavy Dow names (UNH, V, KO, MCD) and rate-sensitive industrials.

factors.name: China Tech Regulation & Foreign Investment Scrutiny
factors.summary: NDRC and regulators questioning AI-fueled stock rallies and demanding disclosure from companies/funds. Supply-chain and growth uncertainty for global tech/industrial supply chains.

factors.name: Upcoming Data & Event Risk
factors.summary: German IFO Business Survey due today; no major US data releases flagged for immediate hours. Weekend approaching with mixed headlines; uncertainty caps conviction ahead of week-end close.

key_levels.resistance: 50,400–50,500 (recent intraday highs, Iran deal euphoria cap)

screening.narrative: US30 opened the week with jitters but has staged a modest recovery (+0.53% in 24h, +0.23% in 4h) on US-Iran peace deal headlines and risk-on sentiment from Asian equity strength. However, confluence of headwinds—rising US dollar (6-week high), persistent oil/energy cost risks (Hormuz closure concerns), European stagflation weakness, and hawkish Fed rate expectations—limits upside. Dow's 50% international revenue exposure and heavy industrial weighting (CAT, BA, GE sensitivity to trade/energy costs) make it vulnerable to both geopolitical escalation and economic slowdown narratives.
catalysts_ahead: German IFO Business Climate Survey (May 22, imminent) — key real-economy read for eurozone demand
US-Iran deal announcement (expected imminently but delayed; any headline swing could trigger volatility)
FOMC decision / Powell comments (timing TBD, but markets pricing hawkish bias)
Dow component earnings (MSFT, UNH, GS, JPM ongoing) — real-time guidance on cost pressures and demand
China economic data (PMI, GDP) — Caterpillar, Boeing, materials demand sensitivity
Oil price move (WTI $95–100 range; Hormuz resolution could trigger 5–10% swing)
screening.risk_warning: A concrete US-Iran deal announcement (or Hormuz reopening) would invalidate bearish oil/stagflation thesis and trigger sharp rally, especially if it stabilizes energy costs and reduces recession fears. Conversely, escalation, uranium enrichment standoff, or tariff/trade war resurgence would accelerate downside. Fed dovish pivot (inflation surprise cool) would also reverse hawkish dollar/rate setup. Current phase is choppy, waiting for clarity.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing; move is +0.5% in 24h (early Phase 2, not extended). Wait for retest of 50,100–50,150 support on profit-taking dip before considering long entry, or hold for post-weekend clarity on Iran deal status. Sell into 50,400–50,500 resistance if it tags without follow-through.
